questions.stem: The book _____ by many students in our school last year.
questions.explanation: 本题考查一般过去时的被动语态。句中时间状语'last year'（去年）是过去时间的标志，因此要用一般过去时；主语'The book'与动词'read'之间是被动关系（书被读），所以要用被动语态'was/were + 过去分词'的结构。A选项'is read'是一般现在时被动语态，用于经常性、习惯性的动作；C选项'reads'是主动语态；D选项'reading'是现在分词，不能单独作谓语。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem:
— _____ you ever _____ to the Shenzhen Science Museum?
—Yes, I _____ there last weekend.
questions.explanation: 本题考查现在完成时与一般过去时的区别。第一空根据'ever'（曾经）可知要用现在完成时'Have...been'，表示'去过某地（已回）'；'have gone to'表示'去了某地（未回）'，不符合语境。第二空根据'last weekend'（上周末）这一具体的过去时间状语，必须用一般过去时'went'。口诀：'ever/never/already/yet'用现完，'yesterday/last week/...ago'用过去。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The girl _____ is singing on the stage is my classmate.
questions.explanation: 本题考查定语从句关系代词的选择。先行词'The girl'指人，排除指物的B选项'which'。分析从句结构，'_____ is singing'中缺少主语，因此要用主格'who'。C选项'whom'是宾格，在从句中作宾语时使用；D选项'whose'表示'谁的'，在从句中作定语。技巧：指人用who/whom/that，指物用which/that，缺主语用who/which，缺宾语用whom/which/who。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem:
—Is that Mr. Wang knocking at the door?
—It _____ be him. He has gone to Shanghai on business.
questions.explanation: 本题考查情态动词表推测的用法。根据答句'He has gone to Shanghai'（他去上海出差了，现在还没回来），可以非常确定敲门的人不可能是王老师。表示'有把握的否定推测'要用'can't'（不可能）。A选项'must'表示'一定是'，用于肯定推测；B选项'might'表示'可能是'，把握性较小；D选项'should'表示'应该'。口诀：肯定推测用must，否定推测用can't，不确定用may/might。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: We should _____ the environment by saving water and reducing waste.
questions.explanation: 本题考查动词词义辨析。A选项'protect'意为'保护'；B选项'prevent'意为'阻止'，常与'from'搭配；C选项'pollute'意为'污染'；D选项'produce'意为'生产'。根据句意'通过节水和减少浪费来_____环境'，可知应该是'保护环境'。解题技巧：根据语境中的'saving water and reducing waste'（正面行为）可推断出需要填入一个积极的动词。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: Please _____ your old clothes to the charity instead of throwing them away.
questions.explanation: 本题考查动词短语辨析。A选项'give away'意为'捐赠；赠送'；B选项'give up'意为'放弃'；C选项'give out'意为'分发；耗尽'；D选项'give in'意为'屈服；让步'。根据句中的'charity'（慈善机构）和'instead of throwing them away'（而不是扔掉），可知此处表示'捐赠旧衣服'。技巧：give away侧重'无偿给予'，give out侧重'向多人分发'。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: It is _____ to drive after drinking alcohol. It's against the law.
questions.explanation: 本题考查词性转换及词义。A选项'legal'是形容词，意为'合法的'；B选项'illegal'是形容词，意为'非法的'（il-为否定前缀）；C选项'legally'是副词；D选项'legality'是名词。根据'It's against the law'（这是违法的）可知，酒驾是'非法的'。句型'It is + adj. + to do sth.'中需要填入形容词，且根据语境需要否定含义。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.explanation: 本题考查名词辨析及语境理解。根据固定搭配'the importance of...'（……的重要性）以及句意'帮助学生理解保护地球的重要性'，应选A。B选项'different'是形容词；C选项'difficult'（困难的）和D选项'interesting'（有趣的）虽然语法上可行，但不如'importance'符合'理解……的重要性'这一常用表达。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school launched an environmental project called "Green Campus Action". The main goal was to help students understand the (11)_____ of protecting our planet through practical activities.

Each class was required to create an artwork using recyclable materials. My classmates and I decided to make a model of the Earth using old newspapers and plastic bottles. At first, we thought it would be an (12)_____ task, but we soon realized it was quite challenging. We spent three weekends working on it. During the process, we had to (13)_____ different methods several times to make the structure stable.

On the day of the exhibition, many students (14)_____ around our artwork and asked questions about how we made it. Some even wanted to learn the skills from us. When our class won the first prize, everyone cheered with excitement.

This experience not only improved our teamwork skills but also made us deeply understand that environmental protection starts with small actions in our daily life. We plan to (15)_____ such activities and encourage more students to join us.

questions.explanation: 本题考查形容词辨析及逻辑关系。根据后文的'but we soon realized it was quite challenging'（但我们很快意识到它很有挑战性）可知，此处表示转折，前面应该是'以为很容易'。因此选A'easy'。B选项'hard'与后文重复，无法形成转折；C选项'important'和D选项'boring'与'challenging'的对比关系不如'easy'明显。解题关键：抓住'but'这一转折连词。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school launched an environmental project called "Green Campus Action". The main goal was to help students understand the (11)_____ of protecting our planet through practical activities.

Each class was required to create an artwork using recyclable materials. My classmates and I decided to make a model of the Earth using old newspapers and plastic bottles. At first, we thought it would be an (12)_____ task, but we soon realized it was quite challenging. We spent three weekends working on it. During the process, we had to (13)_____ different methods several times to make the structure stable.

On the day of the exhibition, many students (14)_____ around our artwork and asked questions about how we made it. Some even wanted to learn the skills from us. When our class won the first prize, everyone cheered with excitement.

This experience not only improved our teamwork skills but also made us deeply understand that environmental protection starts with small actions in our daily life. We plan to (15)_____ such activities and encourage more students to join us.

questions.explanation: 本题考查动词辨析及固定搭配。根据语境'我们不得不多次_____不同的方法'，应选A'try'（尝试）。'try different methods'是固定搭配，意为'尝试不同的方法'。B选项'make'（制作）；C选项'buy'（购买）；D选项'sell'（出售）均不符合语境。前文提到'quite challenging'（有挑战性），因此需要不断'尝试'。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school launched an environmental project called "Green Campus Action". The main goal was to help students understand the (11)_____ of protecting our planet through practical activities.

Each class was required to create an artwork using recyclable materials. My classmates and I decided to make a model of the Earth using old newspapers and plastic bottles. At first, we thought it would be an (12)_____ task, but we soon realized it was quite challenging. We spent three weekends working on it. During the process, we had to (13)_____ different methods several times to make the structure stable.

On the day of the exhibition, many students (14)_____ around our artwork and asked questions about how we made it. Some even wanted to learn the skills from us. When our class won the first prize, everyone cheered with excitement.

This experience not only improved our teamwork skills but also made us deeply understand that environmental protection starts with small actions in our daily life. We plan to (15)_____ such activities and encourage more students to join us.

questions.explanation: 本题考查动词辨析及语境理解。根据语境'许多学生_____在我们的作品周围并询问如何制作的'，应选B'gathered'（聚集）。A选项'stood'（站立）虽然语法正确，但'gather around'是更地道的搭配，表示'围聚'；C选项'slept'（睡觉）和D选项'shouted'（喊叫）不符合语境。后文'asked questions'和'learn the skills'暗示学生们是'围聚'过来观看。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school launched an environmental project called "Green Campus Action". The main goal was to help students understand the (11)_____ of protecting our planet through practical activities.

Each class was required to create an artwork using recyclable materials. My classmates and I decided to make a model of the Earth using old newspapers and plastic bottles. At first, we thought it would be an (12)_____ task, but we soon realized it was quite challenging. We spent three weekends working on it. During the process, we had to (13)_____ different methods several times to make the structure stable.

On the day of the exhibition, many students (14)_____ around our artwork and asked questions about how we made it. Some even wanted to learn the skills from us. When our class won the first prize, everyone cheered with excitement.

This experience not only improved our teamwork skills but also made us deeply understand that environmental protection starts with small actions in our daily life. We plan to (15)_____ such activities and encourage more students to join us.

questions.explanation: 本题考查动词辨析及逻辑关系。根据后文'encourage more students to join us'（鼓励更多学生加入），可知我们计划'继续'这类活动，因此选B'continue'。A选项'stop'（停止）和D选项'refuse'（拒绝）与后文逻辑相反；C选项'forget'（忘记）不符合语境。前文提到'This experience not only improved...'（这次经历不仅提高了……），暗示我们希望'继续'做下去。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:

questions.stem: What did Peesapaty use to make the edible spoons?
options: B. Rice flour, wheat flour and water
questions.explanation: 本题考查细节理解。根据文章第二段最后一句'he finally succeeded by using a special combination of rice flour, wheat flour and water'（他最终通过使用米粉、面粉和水的特殊混合物取得了成功），可知正确答案为B。A选项提到'plastic'（塑料），与文章主题'替代塑料'相悖；C选项'wood'（木头）文中未提及；D选项'Ginger and cinnamon'（姜和肉桂）是香料口味，不是制作材料。解题技巧：细节题要精准定位关键词'ingredients'或'recipes'所在的句子。
questions.label: 阅读-细节理解
questions.group_passage:
Do you know that plastic cutlery (餐具) is a major source of pollution? Every year, millions of plastic spoons, forks and knives are thrown away after just one use. To solve this problem, an Indian inventor named Narayana Peesapaty created an interesting alternative—the edible spoon.

Peesapaty spent years testing different recipes to find the right mixture of ingredients. He wanted to make sure the spoons were strong enough to hold hot soup for at least 20 minutes without getting soggy (变软). After many failures, he finally succeeded by using a special combination of rice flour, wheat flour and water.

These edible spoons come in different flavors like ginger, cinnamon and black pepper. They are 100% natural and biodegradable (可生物降解的). If you don't want to eat them, they will naturally decompose within four to five days.

Since 2010, Peesapaty's company has sold millions of these spoons. Many restaurants and food companies have started using them instead of plastic ones. "The best way to reduce waste is to make products that don't create waste in the first place," Peesapaty said.

questions.stem: Why did Peesapaty spend years testing different recipes?
options: A. Because he wanted to make different flavors
B. Because he needed to find a strong and practical mixture
C. Because he had no other work to do
D. Because he enjoyed cooking very much
questions.explanation: 本题考查推理判断。根据第二段第二句'He wanted to make sure the spoons were strong enough to hold hot soup for at least 20 minutes without getting soggy'（他想确保勺子足够坚固，能盛放热汤至少20分钟而不变软），可推断出他花费多年测试配方是为了找到坚固且实用的混合物。A选项'制作不同口味'是后来添加的特性，不是测试多年的原因；C和D选项文中未提及且不符合逻辑。技巧：推理题要基于文中事实进行合理推断，不能过度推理或选择文中未提及的信息。
questions.label: 阅读-推理判断
questions.group_passage:
Do you know that plastic cutlery (餐具) is a major source of pollution? Every year, millions of plastic spoons, forks and knives are thrown away after just one use. To solve this problem, an Indian inventor named Narayana Peesapaty created an interesting alternative—the edible spoon.

Peesapaty spent years testing different recipes to find the right mixture of ingredients. He wanted to make sure the spoons were strong enough to hold hot soup for at least 20 minutes without getting soggy (变软). After many failures, he finally succeeded by using a special combination of rice flour, wheat flour and water.

These edible spoons come in different flavors like ginger, cinnamon and black pepper. They are 100% natural and biodegradable (可生物降解的). If you don't want to eat them, they will naturally decompose within four to five days.

Since 2010, Peesapaty's company has sold millions of these spoons. Many restaurants and food companies have started using them instead of plastic ones. "The best way to reduce waste is to make products that don't create waste in the first place," Peesapaty said.

questions.stem: The underlined word "decompose" in Paragraph 3 probably means "_____".
questions.explanation: 本题考查词义猜测。根据上下文'If you don't want to eat them, they will naturally decompose within four to five days'（如果你不想吃它们，它们会在四到五天内自然地_____），以及前文提到的'100% natural and biodegradable'（100%天然且可生物降解），可猜测'decompose'意为'自然分解'。A选项'突然消失'过于绝对；C选项'变硬'与语境相反；D选项'产生污染'与文章环保主题相悖。技巧：通过同义词'biodegradable'（可生物降解的）和'naturally'（自然地）可推断词义。
questions.label: 阅读-词义猜测
questions.group_passage:
Do you know that plastic cutlery (餐具) is a major source of pollution? Every year, millions of plastic spoons, forks and knives are thrown away after just one use. To solve this problem, an Indian inventor named Narayana Peesapaty created an interesting alternative—the edible spoon.

Peesapaty spent years testing different recipes to find the right mixture of ingredients. He wanted to make sure the spoons were strong enough to hold hot soup for at least 20 minutes without getting soggy (变软). After many failures, he finally succeeded by using a special combination of rice flour, wheat flour and water.

These edible spoons come in different flavors like ginger, cinnamon and black pepper. They are 100% natural and biodegradable (可生物降解的). If you don't want to eat them, they will naturally decompose within four to five days.

Since 2010, Peesapaty's company has sold millions of these spoons. Many restaurants and food companies have started using them instead of plastic ones. "The best way to reduce waste is to make products that don't create waste in the first place," Peesapaty said.

questions.stem: What is the best title for this passage?
options: A. How to Cook Indian Food
B. The History of Plastic Pollution
C. An Edible Solution to Plastic Waste
D. The Life of an Indian Inventor
questions.explanation: 本题考查主旨大意/最佳标题。文章主要介绍了一种可食用的勺子作为塑料餐具的环保替代品，因此C选项'解决塑料垃圾的可食用方案'最能概括全文主旨。A选项'如何烹饪印度食物'只涉及第三段的口味，太片面；B选项'塑料污染的历史'只在第一段提及，非重点；D选项'一位印度发明家的生平'过于宽泛，文章重点是发明物而非人物生平。技巧：最佳标题要涵盖文章核心话题（edible spoon）和目的（solve plastic pollution），避免以偏概全或过于宽泛。
questions.label: 阅读-主旨大意
questions.group_passage:
Do you know that plastic cutlery (餐具) is a major source of pollution? Every year, millions of plastic spoons, forks and knives are thrown away after just one use. To solve this problem, an Indian inventor named Narayana Peesapaty created an interesting alternative—the edible spoon.

Peesapaty spent years testing different recipes to find the right mixture of ingredients. He wanted to make sure the spoons were strong enough to hold hot soup for at least 20 minutes without getting soggy (变软). After many failures, he finally succeeded by using a special combination of rice flour, wheat flour and water.

These edible spoons come in different flavors like ginger, cinnamon and black pepper. They are 100% natural and biodegradable (可生物降解的). If you don't want to eat them, they will naturally decompose within four to five days.

Since 2010, Peesapaty's company has sold millions of these spoons. Many restaurants and food companies have started using them instead of plastic ones. "The best way to reduce waste is to make products that don't create waste in the first place," Peesapaty said.

options: A. However, it wasn't easy at the beginning.
B. Then I came up with a solution.
C. As a result, my grades improved and I became more confident.
D. One day, I decided to ask for help.
E. In addition, I set specific hours for basketball practice.
questions.explanation: 本题考查段落衔接与逻辑关系。根据空格后一句'I talked to my teacher about my problem'（我和老师谈了我的问题），可知前文应该是'决定寻求帮助'，因此选D。A选项表示转折，B选项表示提出解决方案，C选项表示结果，均与后文'向老师求助'的语义衔接不紧密。技巧：关注后文动作的触发点，'talked to teacher'对应'ask for help'。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
As a middle school student, I used to struggle with managing my time between studying and hobbies. I loved playing basketball, but my grades started to drop because I spent too much time on the court. I felt stressed and didn't know what to do.

(17)_____ I talked to my teacher about my problem. She suggested that I should make a schedule and stick to it.

(18)_____ I decided to finish all my homework first before going to practice. This way, I wouldn't worry about unfinished assignments while playing.

(19)_____ I found that having a clear plan actually improved my efficiency. I could focus better on both studying and sports.

(20)_____ Now I not only get good grades but also enjoy my basketball games more. Balancing study and hobbies is possible if we manage our time wisely.

options: A. However, it wasn't easy at the beginning.
B. Then I came up with a solution.
C. As a result, my grades improved and I became more confident.
D. One day, I decided to ask for help.
E. In addition, I set specific hours for basketball practice.
questions.explanation: 本题考查段落衔接。前文提到老师建议制定时间表，后文'I decided to finish all my homework first...'（我决定先完成作业……）是具体的解决方案，因此空格处应选B'然后我想出了一个解决方案'，起到承上启下的作用。A选项表转折，C选项表结果，D选项已用过，E选项是补充措施而非总体方案。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
As a middle school student, I used to struggle with managing my time between studying and hobbies. I loved playing basketball, but my grades started to drop because I spent too much time on the court. I felt stressed and didn't know what to do.

(17)_____ I talked to my teacher about my problem. She suggested that I should make a schedule and stick to it.

(18)_____ I decided to finish all my homework first before going to practice. This way, I wouldn't worry about unfinished assignments while playing.

(19)_____ I found that having a clear plan actually improved my efficiency. I could focus better on both studying and sports.

(20)_____ Now I not only get good grades but also enjoy my basketball games more. Balancing study and hobbies is possible if we manage our time wisely.

options: A. However, it wasn't easy at the beginning.
B. Then I came up with a solution.
C. As a result, my grades improved and I became more confident.
D. One day, I decided to ask for help.
E. In addition, I set specific hours for basketball practice.
questions.explanation: 本题考查逻辑关系。前文提到'先完成作业再去练习'，后文说'我发现有清晰的计划实际上提高了效率'，空格处应填入另一个具体措施。E选项'此外，我设定了具体的篮球练习时间'符合语境，'In addition'表示递进，补充另一个时间管理的方法。A选项表转折，与后文'improved'的积极含义不符；C选项是最终结果，应放在更后面。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
As a middle school student, I used to struggle with managing my time between studying and hobbies. I loved playing basketball, but my grades started to drop because I spent too much time on the court. I felt stressed and didn't know what to do.

(17)_____ I talked to my teacher about my teacher about my problem. She suggested that I should make a schedule and stick to it.

(18)_____ I decided to finish all my homework first before going to practice. This way, I wouldn't worry about unfinished assignments while playing.

(19)_____ I found that having a clear plan actually improved my efficiency. I could focus better on both studying and sports.

(20)_____ Now I not only get good grades but also enjoy my basketball games more. Balancing study and hobbies is possible if we manage our time wisely.

options: A. However, it wasn't easy at the beginning.
B. Then I came up with a solution.
C. As a result, my grades improved and I became more confident.
D. One day, I decided to ask for help.
E. In addition, I set specific hours for basketball practice.
questions.explanation: 本题考查因果关系。根据后文'Now I not only get good grades...'（现在我不仅取得好成绩……），可知空格处应填入表示结果的过渡句。C选项'As a result'（因此，结果是）引出前文措施带来的积极成果，与后文的具体描述'get good grades'形成呼应。A选项表转折，B选项表方案，D、E已用过。技巧：关注'As a result'、'Therefore'、'So'等表示因果关系的连接词。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
As a middle school student, I used to struggle with managing my time between studying and hobbies. I loved playing basketball, but my grades started to drop because I spent too much time on the court. I felt stressed and didn't know what to do.

(17)_____ I talked to my teacher about my problem. She suggested that I should make a schedule and stick to it.

(18)_____ I decided to finish all my homework first before going to practice. This way, I wouldn't worry about unfinished assignments while playing.

(19)_____ I found that having a clear plan actually improved my efficiency. I could focus better on both studying and sports.

(20)_____ Now I not only get good grades but also enjoy my basketball games more. Balancing study and hobbies is possible if we manage our time wisely.
